trade.summary: Spain and Poland have growing bilateral trade as major EU economies. Spain exports food products, vehicles, and chemicals; Poland exports machinery, furniture, vehicles, and food products. Both benefit from EU single market access. Poland is Spain's most important Eastern European trade partner.

diplomatic.status: EU partners with improving relations under Tusk
diplomatic.summary: Spain and Poland are EU members with generally good but not always aligned positions. Under the Tusk government (2023-), relations improved significantly as Poland moved back toward EU mainstream positions. Both cooperate on Ukraine support. They differ somewhat on migration policy (Poland more restrictive, Spain more open). Both are NATO allies.

military.summary: Both NATO members. Spain has contributed to NATO Enhanced Forward Presence missions in Poland's neighborhood. Poland is one of NATO's most important eastern flank states with rising defense spending. Both cooperate on Ukraine military assistance within NATO frameworks.

financial.summary: Moderate bilateral investment. Spanish banks (Santander, BBVA) have some Polish operations. Spanish infrastructure firms are active in Poland (benefiting from EU cohesion funds). Polish investment in Spain is minimal.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Poland is coal-dependent and transitioning; Spain is a renewable leader. Some potential for Spanish renewable electricity (via interconnectors) to eventually reach Poland. Spain's LNG expertise could benefit Polish LNG import ambitions.

scientific.summary: Active scientific cooperation through EU Horizon programs. Spanish and Polish universities collaborate in physics, chemistry, and environmental sciences. Large Polish community in Spain contributes to people-to-people ties. Erasmus program is extensively used between the two countries.
